dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 10.43 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Per-tool enable/disable for every MCP connector. Open Settings → MCP, click the new purple "Tools (k/n)" pill on any connector row to expand a categorised list — Essential tools at the top with a green badge, Common in the middle, Advanced (writes, destructive) at the bottom with a grey badge. Each row carries a one-line description and an on/off toggle. Disabled tools are stripped from `tools/list` entirely, dropping their schema bytes from Claude Code's system prompt and blocking calls. Toggling one auto-restarts running `claude` sessions so the change takes effect immediately. Catalog covers all 85 currently-shipped MCP tools across Slack, ClickUp, Google (Drive / Gmail / Calendar), GitHub, GitLab, and Bitwarden. Settings → General → Popover layout now auto-opens the menu-bar popover when you pick a layout, so you can preview Full / Standard / Tiny side-by-side with the settings window and decide without committing.
